_disclaimer: SPECULATIVE discretionary research, NOT a backtested edge. Live snapshot, not point-in-time. Individual-stock survivorship applies. Prices/targets/ratings/estimates are live Bigdata.com tearsheet data (quotes as of 2026-06-26 close); drawdown & base-position are from the live 52-week range. ROE figures marked ~ are approximate where a point-in-time ratio was unavailable. Research leads, not signals — manual trades only.

ideas.thesis: Salesforce sits at a 3-year low (-43% off its high) yet just printed a Q1 FY27 beat: net income +37% YoY, revenue +13% to $11.13B, cRPO +14%, and $6.6B free cash flow. The entire 42% de-rate is sentiment — an 'AI will cannibalize SaaS seats' fear ('SaaSpocalypse') — not a fundamentals breakdown, leaving the stock at ~12x forward EPS with 75 of 97 analysts at Buy/Strong-Buy and +68% to consensus. If Agentforce/AI monetization (deals up ~60%) outruns the seat erosion it is feared to cause, this is the cleanest beaten-down compounder in the cohort.
ideas.key_risk: The AI-cannibalization fear is structural, not imaginary — agentic AI could compress the per-seat subscription model faster than Agentforce monetizes, and the $3.6B Fin acquisition hints growth may have to be bought rather than earned.

news.headline: Salesforce Stock Is at a 3-Year Low. Should Investors Consider Buying? (rev & EPS topped estimates, $6.6B FCF)
Salesforce CEO Brushes Off 'SaaSpocalypse' As CRM Slides Despite 60% Surge In Premium AI Deals
Salesforce Likely to Meet Muted Quarterly Views Amid 'Tenuous' SaaS Backdrop; risk/reward 'leans favorably'

competitors.name: ServiceNow
competitors.note: Faster grower but ~2x CRM's multiple; both sold off on the same AI-SaaS fear.

competitors.name: Adobe
competitors.note: Cheaper and higher-margin, but slower-growing and more directly AI-exposed.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Dynamics)
competitors.note: Approx; the deep-pocketed competitive threat, but priced for perfection vs CRM.

ideas.competitor_edge: Cheapest large-cap SaaS on growth-adjusted terms (~12x fwd EPS for a 13% grower with +14% cRPO and $6.6B FCF), with the widest analyst conviction and upside of the software trio — you are paying a value multiple for a still-compounding franchise.

ideas.thesis: ServiceNow is the most beaten-down name in the cohort (-53% off its high, -52% over 1Y) despite being, fundamentally, the highest-quality: a durable ~20% revenue grower at ~35% FCF margins, 97% renewal rate, used by 85% of the Fortune 500. The April 12% post-earnings drop was, per Canaccord, a 'punitive reaction to a quarter that seemed fine' (a one-off Middle-East on-prem deal slip), and the rest is premium-multiple compression from higher bond yields. Nvidia's Jensen Huang publicly calls NOW the 'enterprise operating system for AI', so the agentic-AI wave that scares investors about SaaS is arguably a tailwind here.
ideas.key_risk: It still carries the richest multiple in the group (~22x forward EPS), so even with the de-rate it has the furthest to fall if enterprise IT budgets tighten or the in-line (non-beat) earnings cadence persists.

news.headline: ServiceNow: An Excellent Business Trading at a Discount (Nvidia's Huang calls it the 'enterprise OS for AI'; 97% renewal)
Canaccord cuts ServiceNow target but calls shares 'ridiculously cheap'; 'durable 20% growth at 35% FCF margins'
ServiceNow Shares Sliding as market turns risk-off; premium software multiples hit by higher yields

competitors.name: Salesforce
competitors.note: Cheaper and bigger FCF, but growing slower than NOW's ~20%.

competitors.name: Workday
competitors.note: Approx; similar multiple, lower growth and weaker moat than NOW.

competitors.name: Adobe
competitors.note: Much cheaper, but a maturing franchise vs NOW's 20% top-line.

ideas.competitor_edge: Best-in-class growth quality of the cohort — ~20% organic growth, 97% renewals, 85% F500 penetration and an explicit Nvidia endorsement — now available 53% below its high; the multiple is still premium but the business quality is the most defensible.

ideas.thesis: Netflix is at a 20-month low (-45% off its high) even though its last print was a 61% EPS beat — Q1 revenue +16% to $12.25B, operating margin 32.3%, net income $5.28B — the sharpest 'market is wrong' disconnect in the cohort. The sell-off is about narrative, not numbers: a soft Q2 guide, co-founder Reed Hastings stepping back, and an out-of-character M&A pivot (a lost $22B Roku bid, interest in Warner Bros. Discovery/Lionsgate) that has investors fearing the best growth is behind it. The only consistently profitable scaled streamer, trading at ~22x forward EPS with +51% to consensus.
ideas.key_risk: The deceleration is real, not just sentiment — M Science flags the weakest net-adds quarter since 2022 with US churn rising, and a sudden appetite for expensive acquisitions can signal that organic growth is maturing.

news.headline: Netflix quarterly sales beat but soft outlook + Reed Hastings to step down send shares lower (EPS $1.23 vs $0.76; op margin 32.3%)
Netflix Stock Sinks To 52-Week Low on M&A anxiety after losing $22B Roku bid to Fox
Netflix slips as Meta expands Instagram for TV; M Science warns weakest net adds since 2022, churn picking up

competitors.name: Disney (streaming)
competitors.note: Approx; streaming only recently profitable, far lower margins than NFLX.

competitors.name: Warner Bros. Discovery
competitors.note: Approx; a debt-laden turnaround / takeover target, not a profitable peer.

competitors.name: Spotify
competitors.note: Approx; faster top-line but a fraction of Netflix's margin profile.

ideas.competitor_edge: The only scaled streamer that is durably and highly profitable — 32% operating margins and 16% growth while rivals lose money or carry debt — yet it is priced like a no-growth media name after a 61% earnings beat.

ideas.thesis: Adobe is the deepest-value name on the screen: -48% off its high and trading at roughly 8x forward EPS — a value multiple — for a franchise still growing revenue ~11% (Q2 +11% to $6.62B) at a 37% net margin and ~15% ROE. The de-rate is a single clean bet: that generative AI (Midjourney, Sora-style tools) structurally erodes Creative Cloud. Management is responding by leaning in — pausing Creative Cloud price hikes to build a freemium AI funnel and launching Firefly Foundry (custom enterprise AI models) — which depresses near-term ARR but is the right defense if it works.
ideas.key_risk: The disruption thesis is the most existential in the cohort — genAI substitutes directly for Adobe's core product, and pausing price increases to chase free users is a tacit admission that pricing power is under pressure, which could cap the +28% upside that is already the lowest in the group.

news.headline: Adobe Pauses Price Hikes For 'Short-Term' AI Push - But At What Cost To ARR Growth? (-5% overnight; Q2 rev +11%)
Adobe Introduces Firefly Foundry to Support Custom AI Models for Enterprise Brand Consistency

competitors.name: Salesforce
competitors.note: Faster grower; both de-rated on AI fear, ADBE the higher-margin and cheaper.

competitors.name: ServiceNow
competitors.note: Nearly 3x Adobe's multiple for a less profitable model.

competitors.name: Figma / Canva (private)
competitors.note: Approx; the design-tool insurgents pressuring Adobe's core, not public comps.

ideas.competitor_edge: Cheapest of the entire screen (~8x forward EPS) with the highest margins (37%) and strong FCF — if the market is even partly wrong about AI substitution, the re-rating from a value multiple back toward a normal software multiple is large.

ideas.thesis: Nike is the textbook beaten-down turnaround — sitting essentially ON its 52-week low (1.9% off the bottom, -49% off its high) under returning CEO Elliott Hill, who is cleaning up the botched DTC pivot, resetting China, and rebuilding wholesale. It beat a low Q3 bar (+20% EPS surprise) and BofA expects gross-margin expansion to begin in Q2 FY27 as tariffs subside. But this is a CAUTION, not a CONFIRM: net margins have collapsed to ~6% (from a historical ~11%), forward EPS is choppy and below prior-year, and management itself admits 'the turnaround is taking longer than expected.'
ideas.key_risk: The recovery is unproven and the clock is real — China is modeled down ~20% in Q4, On and Hoka keep taking share, and analysts have pushed the demand inflection out to 2027, so the rock-bottom base could persist (or deepen) before margins actually turn.

news.headline: Nike CEO Elliott Hill Admits Turnaround Is Taking Longer Than Expected: 'We Still Have Work To Do'
Nike price target lowered to $55 from $75 at BTIG (Buy kept); Q4 Greater China modeled ~-20%
Why This Analyst Thinks Nike Guidance Matters More Than Earnings; gross-margin expansion seen starting Q2 FY27

competitors.name: On Holding
competitors.note: Approx; taking share from Nike with ~30% growth — the share-loss problem in one line.

competitors.name: Deckers (HOKA)
competitors.note: Approx; HOKA eroding Nike's running franchise at much higher margins.

competitors.name: Adidas
competitors.note: Approx; further along its own recovery, regaining shelf Nike vacated.

ideas.competitor_edge: Honestly, none right now — Nike's edge is latent (scale, brand, distribution) and currently being eroded by On and Hoka; the bet is on a margin/brand-heat recovery that has not yet shown up in the numbers, which is exactly why this is a watch, not a buy.

ideas.thesis: AT&T is a fine, stable income stock — fiber to 37M locations (40M by year-end, 60M by 2030), $45B of dividends + buybacks planned for 2026-28, a maintained $1.11 dividend, and reaffirmed guidance — but it does not fit the blow-up mandate, so the honest call is VETO. It is the LEAST beaten-down name the screen flagged (only -24% off its high vs -43% to -53% for the cohort), its forward EPS is flat at ~$2.30 with legacy revenue still declining 20%+, and it is the only name in the cohort that analysts rate a consensus HOLD (more holds than buys).
ideas.key_risk: Structurally it cannot deliver an asymmetric move — beta 0.395 makes it a bond-proxy, so the fingerprint flagged it on an ordinary low-vol pullback rather than a coiled turnaround; rising rates, not company decline, are the main swing factor.

news.headline: AT&T Rides Fiber Boom While Legacy Business Fades (Legacy revenue guided -20%+ in 2026; fiber to 40M by year-end)
AT&T reiterates 2026 and multi-year guidance; $45B+ shareholder returns 2026-28, deleveraging toward 2.5x
AT&T Expands Fiber Push With $19 Billion Statewide Infrastructure Plan In California

competitors.name: Verizon
competitors.note: Approx; near-identical low-growth income profile, higher yield.

competitors.name: T-Mobile
competitors.note: Approx; the actual telecom growth leader — where the asymmetric upside in the sector sits, not T.

ideas.competitor_edge: None relevant to this mandate — within telecom T is cheap and well-run, but it is a low-beta income/deleveraging story; the 'blow-up' potential the screen hunts for simply isn't in a 0.4-beta bond proxy, so it is excluded rather than recommended.
